title: ‘We were at their mercy’: inside the Amazon tactics that hiked prices across the internet
excerpt: Internal emails revealed in court and interviews with ex-employees describe how Amazon’s practices drove up prices for competitors like Walmart and Target. Amazon denies price-fixing claims and says it works to lower costs for consumers
content_html:
A “modern leather” table lamp, listed online at Walmart, shot up from $24.99 to $39.

The price of an air fryer on a retail site called Newegg went from $84.99 to $149.99.

An electric ice-cream maker was listed at just $17.99 at Amazon and Best Buy. Then it became unavailable at Best Buy, and more than tripled in price to $59.99 at Amazon.

The price hikes and the product disappearances were not the blips of supply and demand – they were the results of behind-the-scenes pressure by Amazon, according to internal emails reviewed by the Guardian and court claims against the world’s largest company.

For years, as many Americans have felt squeezed by the costs of basic consumer goods, Amazon has deployed a variety of techniques that have incentivized suppliers to push for higher product prices on other retailers’ sites, California authorities claim in court.

In some internal emails, Amazon employees have flagged low product prices on rival retailers’ sites as threats to Amazon’s own profitability, and informed the products’ suppliers that their sales on Amazon.com have been cut – or might soon be cut – going forward.

Other emails describe Amazon slashing its product prices to match retail rivals like Walmart and Home Depot, then pushing suppliers to compensate it for the revenue lost due to these lower prices.

To escape this financial pressure, some suppliers have responded with a fast and cheap alternative: making sure the prices of their products are higher with Amazon’s rivals, or pulling their products from those rivals altogether, according to internal records cited in litigation.

Over the last decade, Amazon has suppressed the sales of some suppliers’ products or demanded millions of dollars in compensation from them because of the low prices of Amazon’s retail rivals, according to a trove of internal company emails, presentations and notes reviewed by the Guardian, as well as interviews with 15 current or former Amazon supplier representatives, company employees and state and federal law enforcers.

The mass of internal Amazon records were obtained from Amazon by California’s attorney general as part of a lawsuit that alleges the company has engaged in widespread price fixing, which is defined as arrangements among companies that seek to raise or otherwise control the prices of products or services.

The Guardian examined those records, as well as hundreds of pages of deposition testimony of current and former employees of Amazon and supplier companies. Most of the documents and depositions – which were partially unsealed in recent months – have never been publicly reported on.

The California attorney general, Rob Bonta, alleges that Amazon’s tactics have coerced suppliers into raising the prices of their products with its rivals.

In the case of the ice-cream maker, Bonta’s office points to emails that say Amazon temporarily took “down” the supplier’s inventory off its platform – a move that prompted its manufacturer, a firm called Maxi-Matic, to scramble to pull its inventory from Best Buy, eliminating Amazon’s price competition from the electronics retailer.

By the end of that day, Amazon had put Maxi-Matic’s product “back live” on Amazon.com and more than tripled its price, the records show.

In the case of the air fryer, Amazon “suppressed” the product’s sales on its platform and told its supplier that it would stop ordering several of its products for Amazon.com – unless the supplier agreed to reimburse Amazon for revenues the online retailer had lost due to its practice of copying competitors’ lower prices, according to emails revealed through the attorney general’s lawsuit.

This spurred the supplier, a kitchen appliance firm called Chefman, to move to secure a price increase for the air fryer at Target and an online retailer called Newegg as it hashed out an agreement to reimburse Amazon $100,000 on top of the $400,000 it had already given back, according to the emails.

Asked about the air fryer’s price jump, Mark Friedman, Chefman’s senior vice-president of global sales, told the Guardian: “I can’t talk to you because I still do business with Amazon and I don’t want to bite the hand that feeds me.”

Amazon’s written responses to the Guardian did not respond to questions about these specific incidents. The company accuses the California attorney general of “distorting a handful of emails” out of “nearly one million vendor communications” to “suggest a far-reaching conspiracy among unspecified thousands of vendors and retailers”.

Amazon says it works hard to protect customers from “paying too much” and to negotiate economic terms that allow it “to offer products at the competitive prices” that “customers expect from Amazon”. It also says the California attorney general’s suit is “asking the court to prevent Amazon from negotiating with its vendors to lower prices, likely leading to higher prices for Amazon’s customers”.

The incident involving the price hike for the leather table lamp, for example, was part of a broader exchange about dozens of products on Amazon.com sourced from a New Jersey based supplier called All The Rages.

The email exchange shows that Amazon negotiated for cost decreases for many of the supplier’s other products, ensuring lower prices for consumers on Amazon.com.

But the messages also refer to raising the prices of other items sold by Amazon’s competitors.

After Amazon reached out, an All The Rages employee named Joseph Martin replied that his company had “contacted” various unnamed retailers “to fix the retail” and had seen an increase in retail prices “for a lot of the items already”.

Two days later, Martin told Amazon that the lamp price at Walmart had increased from $24.99 to $39. “We should be good now,” he said.

Martin told the Guardian that the lamp’s price change “reflected the correction of a pricing error”.

“Any decision regarding retail pricing was made by Walmart and Amazon, so you would have to ask them about their pricing decisions,” said Martin, who added that his company believes the California attorney general’s claims “are without merit”.

Amazon and Walmart declined to provide statements about this specific incident.

‘It seemed like he knew what was happening’

While such tactics can generate savings for Amazon consumers, they can also raise prices for consumers shopping elsewhere, an effect that company employees on the ground were well aware of, according to supplier representatives as well as former Amazon employees who spoke to the Guardian on the condition of anonymity.

One former vendor manager, who worked at Amazon from 2022 to 2024 before getting laid off, said that on phone calls with suppliers he tried to be upfront about the fact that raising prices with Amazon’s competitors could help them avoid demands that they compensate Amazon for lower-than-anticipated profit margins.

“Hey, if you can make sure this product doesn’t continue to be sold at this lower price point, then we don’t have to keep revisiting this discussion about margin,” the former Amazon vendor manager recalled telling suppliers.

A former Amazon “customer success” manager said that Amazon staff had “very black-and-white conversations” with suppliers about the prospect of raising prices with Amazon’s competitors.

“They’d say, ‘You need to go to Target. You need to go talk to Walmart to get them to raise their price,’” he recalled.

The former customer success manager told the Guardian that higher-ups instructed Amazon employees to have certain conversations with suppliers over the phone in order to avoid a digital trail of potential price-fixing allegations.

“The only rule was, ‘Do not have this in writing’,” he recalled.

The former employees’ accounts echo several Amazon emails and presentations relied on in court by California’s attorney general. One from 2022 reminds staff to “not use email” for certain conversations with suppliers. Another email from 2020 reminds staff to “keep in mind it is often best to have these conversations over the phone”.

Amazon did not directly respond to questions about these sources’ statements and those presentations cited in court by the attorney general. But in legal filings, the company has pointed to other instructions it disseminated to staff barring them from making agreements with vendors or competitors on pricing.

Ryan Turano, chief technology officer of a fertilizer company called AgroThrive, told the Guardian that when Amazon’s profitability was threatened due to its price matching, an Amazon vendor manager would reach out to him and sometimes list off businesses like Walmart or Home Depot for him to contact.

“He’d go, ‘Home Depot is probably causing the problem, talk to your vendor manager there to see what you could do,’” recalled Turano. “It seemed like he knew what was happening.”

In one internal email, that Amazon manager asked Turano for an “update on the price match issue we discussed”. Turano told him that he had “just got out of a meeting with the Home Depot manager,” who had “agreed to raise the prices this time”.

Home Depot declined to comment on the email.

Turano blamed Amazon for the pressure that resulted in price increases like the one he negotiated for with the home improvement retailer.

“We were at their mercy. Because we didn’t have the resources to push back,” Turano said.

“It was just a nightmare. It didn’t feel good,” he added. “It’s almost understandable. Because they’re so big they can do whatever they want.”

Amazon – which recently surpassed Walmart as the world’s biggest company by revenue – is currently facing multiple court cases alleging price-fixing.

Price-fixing lawsuits against Amazon being pursued by the California attorney general and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) are both slated to go to trial in early 2027. The company is also battling similar claims in a private class action lawsuit in federal court in Seattle and a legal action pursued by Washington DC’s attorney general, which was initially dismissed but revived on appeal.

Amazon denies the claims in the various court actions and says its practices encourage lower prices, incentivize competition and create greater product selection for consumers.

In the California case, Amazon told the Guardian that the price fixing theory asserted by the attorney general is “legally untenable, with the state offering no evidence of any communication between Amazon and any other retailer, much less an agreement among them to fix prices. The practices California challenges are common in the retail industry and part of the pro-competitive give-and-take between Amazon and its vendors.”

In 2022 Amazon agreed to pay $2.25m to resolve claims by Washington state’s attorney general that it forced some merchants to raise prices for products they sold through the company’s “Sold by Amazon” plan. The company made no admission of liability and said it strongly believed the program was legal.

The power of algorithms

Amazon has long prided itself on offering Americans the lowest prices online.

The company points to studies showing that over the last nine years, Amazon has consistently offered consumers the lowest prices among major American retailers and academic research suggesting that the competition between online and traditional retailers has driven down product prices and might have actually tamped down on inflation in years past. According to a 2025 study from the analytics firm Profitero+, Amazon’s prices were on average 14% lower than its leading competitors.

But the FTC and California lawsuits allege the company’s vaunted price floor is a mirage, obscuring its behind-the-scenes efforts to drive up its competitors’ prices.

Throughout the 2010s, Amazon systematically mapped the prices of its main retail rivals across the internet, according to testimony of current and former Amazon employees in depositions conducted by the FTC and California authorities.

In 2011, Amazon had a team of about 200 to 250 people using a hybrid mix of manual and automated methods to surveil competitors’ prices, Karthik Mitta, a former Amazon director, testified last year in a deposition in the FTC case. By 2019, Mitta testified, that team had grown to nearly 2,000 people, enabling the company to use web crawlers to collect pricing information and monitor tens of thousands of competitor sites.

Harnessing this knowledge of pricing data across the internet, Amazon adopted what one executive called a “game theory” approach to pricing which could help it evade the costs of a “perfectly competitive market”, according to the FTC’s lawsuit.

Amazon developed an algorithm that rapidly copied rivals’ price changes “to the penny”, according to the FTC. If retail rivals raised or lowered its price, the FTC claimed, Amazon would do the same.

The company did not respond directly to questions regarding allegations about its algorithm, but pointed the Guardian to academic literature and industry reports noting how common it is for retailers to track prices and for suppliers to try to enforce minimum prices for their products.

Arjun Narayan, a former general manager for Amazon’s vendor program, told the Guardian that the company’s price-following tactics were designed to ensure that the company continued to fulfill founder Jeff Bezos’s original mission: that it strive to help customers find “anything they might want to buy online” at “the lowest possible prices”.

“If I figure out that you’re actually selling at a lower price elsewhere, the very promise is defeated, right?” noted Narayan, who now advises brands with his own firm, SalesDuo.

The FTC argues the algorithm sought to instill another lesson among Amazon’s competitors: they could not compete with Amazon by offering lower prices, so with no better options left, they may as well raise their prices – increases that Amazon’s algorithm would then happily follow.

‘We will discontinue from your problematic competition’

In 2020, three days before Black Friday, Amazon informed Armen Living, a California based furniture supplier, that it “had to suppress and remove” one of its office chairs from its platform, according to emails released in the California lawsuit.

Amazon had bought the chair, a minimalist curvy unit with a walnut wood exterior and a chrome-plated steel base, for $79 a pop, and had planned to sell them on its site for $116.45.

But another retailer in the market happened to be selling the same chair for just $58.62, and Amazon had matched that price, which was so low that Amazon was losing money on every sale.

Following the price reduction, Amazon made clear to Armen Living that if it wanted to have the chair “back” up on “the website soon”, it was up to the furniture brand to do something about the financial squeeze caused by Amazon’s own programmatic price matching.

In an email, Paige Nguyen, an Amazon vendor manager, laid out three options that the furniture company could choose from to “help address” the issue:

It could compensate Amazon for the revenue loss – paying Amazon $57.83 for every chair sold on the site.

It could slash the amount it was selling the chairs to Amazon, a future giveaway to the retail colossus.

Or – likely the least costly option – it could “manage” the “other channels” where it sold the chair, a euphemism pointing to the Amazon competitors who were selling the chair for less, according to the California lawsuit.

Nguyen didn’t elaborate on what managing these other “channels” meant, sticking to the kind of language that Amazon uses when training staff on how to talk with suppliers. The California attorney general alleges terms like “channel optimization” are a “naming gloss” for Amazon’s push to get suppliers to enforce minimum prices with or pull inventory from Amazon’s competitors.

Rather than paying compensation or offering a cost decrease to Amazon, Armen Living made clear it understood just how it could manage its channels to appease the online retailer.

The company promised it would “look further into the potential offenders” in the market offering low prices and pull its inventory from those Amazon competitors if need be.

“Amazon is always our top priority,” noted an Armen Living marketing analyst: “… If the problematic retail does not fix by the end of the week, we will discontinue from your problematic competition to ensure that Amazon can return to a healthy state with these items.”

In the end, Armen Living confirmed in an email to Amazon that it had removed its inventory from the “offenders sites”.

Nguyen, the Amazon employee, thanked her for her action, but complained that the prices of two other chairs, which she had also flagged, had “dropped” and “still” not recovered “to the original price”.

“Let me know if you need more time to adjust this,” Nguyen said.

Nguyen did not respond to requests for comment. Amazon and Armen Living did not respond to specific questions about this incident.

Martin Heubel, a consultant for e-commerce brands who previously worked as an Amazon vendor manager, said Amazon’s compensation demands help entrench its power in the market.

“It’s not so much about the money that you recover. It’s more about the education of your suppliers,” said Heubel, who said he was speaking as a consultant who advises brands negotiating with Amazon and not from his experience as a former Amazon employee.

“If you go elsewhere, you will get punished … so think twice about it whether you really do.”

Amazon has said in legal pleadings that the incidents cited in the California case are “alleged to have occurred many years in the past” and involve “employees who lack pricing authority” and “a microscopic portion of Amazon’s catalog of millions of products”.

Confidential presentations

The pressure Amazon exerts on its suppliers may only be partially visible in internal company emails like these because of Amazon’s efforts to train staff on how to avoid putting certain messages in writing, according to a recent court filing by the California attorney general.

The internal records obtained in the California lawsuit include an in-house presentation from 2019. It instructs staff to send screenshots of rivals’ product listings to suppliers when making compensation requests, but to avoid mentioning the companies’ names.

Avoid using competitor name

The former Amazon vendor manager, who asked to speak anonymously, said that he would avoid explicitly telling a supplier that Walmart had caused a price-drop, which Amazon’s algorithm had followed. Instead, he recalled, he would say, “There’s a retailer in blue that sells this product” – a reference to Walmart’s blue, yellow and white color palette.

An Amazon presentation from 2022, also revealed in litigation, instructs staff that it is “not ok” to talk with suppliers about “channel pricing”, an industry term referring to a supplier’s varying prices with different retailers.

do not use email

The same slideshow, however, also gives staff a seemingly contradictory instruction, noting that if they need to highlight “activities in the channel” affecting Amazon’s profitability or if they need to negotiate compensation payments with suppliers, they should “not use email”. It then reminds employees to follow the previously mentioned rules against discussing “channel pricing”.

In an interview with the Guardian, the California attorney general, Bonta, said Amazon’s use of “code” words would not be an adequate defense in court.

“Whatever euphemism it is, whether it be ‘update pricing’ or ‘resolve an issue’ or ‘address a concern’ in the market, all that means is: ‘Fix prices. Get your lower price up to Amazon’s higher price or suffer the consequences,’ and that’s illegal.”

The company rejects that claim. It says the conduct the state of California challenges consists of routine retail contracting and negotiating that “drives down prices for consumers”.

In court, Amazon has pointed to emails and staff presentations showing its compliance with laws protecting free market competition.

In one staff presentation from 2021, for example, the company instructed staff that they should not “permit vendors to share competitor pricing intentions with us”.

Likewise, an email from 2023 shows an Amazon employee telling a vendor representative that Amazon has “no interest in being part” of discussions, “directly or indirectly” with other retailers about their prices.

Despite Amazon’s guidance to staff, representatives for the suppliers themselves said in some email exchanges that they would help raise prices with Amazon’s competitors.

Documents disclosed in litigation show that on the afternoon of 17 February 2023, an Amazon vendor manager named Kara King emailed Andrew Newell, then a manager for an e-commerce company that helped brands interface with Amazon. The Amazon employee wanted Newell to get an appliance company he was representing called RugDoctor to sign a “guaranteed minimum margin” agreement.

The guarantee would ensure it compensated Amazon for a pet-focused vacuum cleaner whose profitability had “been challenged” due to “sales price compression/competitive matching” – a reference to Amazon’s practice of matching its competitors’ prices via algorithm.

But Newell responded that the guarantee wouldn’t be “necessary”. He said he was confident he could “get Walmart to raise the price back up” so that Amazon could then match it. “This would resolve the margin issues,” he wrote.

In response, King, Newell’s counterpart at Amazon, continued to float the possibility of compensation funding, but suggested they not discuss the matter further over email. “This is a delicate conversation for numerous reasons and probably best suited for a phone call/virtual meeting,” she said.

In an interview with the Guardian, Newell said he did not recall the particulars of the incident but said the incentives that Amazon had created for brands made raising prices with competitors an obvious solution.

“It’s kind of like basic marketing understanding and math,” he said.

King did not answer questions from the Guardian about the exchange.

Newell noted that Amazon vendor managers would tell him to schedule price increases with Walmart then reach back out to them, so they could “flip” the price back up “on the same day”. Amazon staffers avoided putting such messages in email, he said.

“They know exactly what they’re doing,” he said, adding, “They’re really well coached. They wanna get you on the phone.”

George Joseph is an investigative reporter for the Guardian. If you currently work for or previously worked for Amazon or an Amazon supplier, reach out to him with tips and insights via text or the encrypted messaging app Signal at 929-486-4865 or via email at george.joseph@theguardian.com.

Or – likely the least costly option – it could “manage” the “other channels” where it sold the chair, a euphemism pointing to the Amazon competitors who were selling the chair for less, according to the California lawsuit.Nguyen didn’t elaborate on what managing these other “channels” meant, sticking to the kind of language that Amazon uses when training staff on how to talk with suppliers. The California attorney general alleges terms like “channel optimization” are a “naming gloss” for Amazon’s push to get suppliers to enforce minimum prices with or pull inventory from Amazon’s competitors.Rather than paying compensation or offering a cost decrease to Amazon, Armen Living made clear it understood just how it could manage its channels to appease the online retailer. The company promised it would “look further into the potential offenders” in the market offering low prices and pull its inventory from those Amazon competitors if need be.“Amazon is always our top priority,” noted an Armen Living marketing analyst: “… If the problematic retail does not fix by the end of the week, we will discontinue from your problematic competition to ensure that Amazon can return to a healthy state with these items.”In the end, Armen Living confirmed in an email to Amazon that it had removed its inventory from the “offenders sites”.Nguyen, the Amazon employee, thanked her for her action, but complained that the prices of two other chairs, which she had also flagged, had “dropped” and “still” not recovered “to the original price”.“Let me know if you need more time to adjust this,” Nguyen said.Nguyen did not respond to requests for comment. Amazon and Armen Living did not respond to specific questions about this incident.Martin Heubel, a consultant for e-commerce brands who previously worked as an Amazon vendor manager, said Amazon’s compensation demands help entrench its power in the market.“It’s not so much about the money that you recover. It’s more about the education of your suppliers,” said Heubel, who said he was speaking as a consultant who advises brands negotiating with Amazon and not from his experience as a former Amazon employee.“If you go elsewhere, you will get punished … so think twice about it whether you really do.”Amazon has said in legal pleadings that the incidents cited in the California case are “alleged to have occurred many years in the past” and involve “employees who lack pricing authority” and “a microscopic portion of Amazon’s catalog of millions of products”.Confidential presentationsThe pressure Amazon exerts on its suppliers may only be partially visible in internal company emails like these because of Amazon’s efforts to train staff on how to avoid putting certain messages in writing, according to a recent court filing by the California attorney general.The internal records obtained in the California lawsuit include an in-house presentation from 2019. It instructs staff to send screenshots of rivals’ product listings to suppliers when making compensation requests, but to avoid mentioning the companies’ names.Excerpt from 2019 Amazon internal presentation released in court by the California attorney general. Photograph: Obtained by The Guardian double quotation markAvoid using competitor name The former Amazon vendor manager, who asked to speak anonymously, said that he would avoid explicitly telling a supplier that Walmart had caused a price-drop, which Amazon’s algorithm had followed. Instead, he recalled, he would say, “There’s a retailer in blue that sells this product” – a reference to Walmart’s blue, yellow and white color palette.An Amazon presentation from 2022, also revealed in litigation, instructs staff that it is “not ok” to talk with suppliers about “channel pricing”, an industry term referring to a supplier’s varying prices with different retailers.Excerpt from 2022 Amazon internal presentation released in court by the California attorney general. Photograph: Obtained by The Guardian double quotation markdo not use email The same slideshow, however, also gives staff a seemingly contradictory instruction, noting that if they need to highlight “activities in the channel” affecting Amazon’s profitability or if they need to negotiate compensation payments with suppliers, they should “not use email”. It then reminds employees to follow the previously mentioned rules against discussing “channel pricing”. In an interview with the Guardian, the California attorney general, Bonta, said Amazon’s use of “code” words would not be an adequate defense in court.“Whatever euphemism it is, whether it be ‘update pricing’ or ‘resolve an issue’ or ‘address a concern’ in the market, all that means is: ‘Fix prices. Get your lower price up to Amazon’s higher price or suffer the consequences,’ and that’s illegal.”The company rejects that claim. It says the conduct the state of California challenges consists of routine retail contracting and negotiating that “drives down prices for consumers”.In court, Amazon has pointed to emails and staff presentations showing its compliance with laws protecting free market competition.A worker delivers packages on Amazon Prime Day in New York, in July 2025. Photograph: Bloomberg/Getty ImagesIn one staff presentation from 2021, for example, the company instructed staff that they should not “permit vendors to share competitor pricing intentions with us”.Likewise, an email from 2023 shows an Amazon employee telling a vendor representative that Amazon has “no interest in being part” of discussions, “directly or indirectly” with other retailers about their prices.Despite Amazon’s guidance to staff, representatives for the suppliers themselves said in some email exchanges that they would help raise prices with Amazon’s competitors.Documents disclosed in litigation show that on the afternoon of 17 February 2023, an Amazon vendor manager named Kara King emailed Andrew Newell, then a manager for an e-commerce company that helped brands interface with Amazon. The Amazon employee wanted Newell to get an appliance company he was representing called RugDoctor to sign a “guaranteed minimum margin” agreement.The guarantee would ensure it compensated Amazon for a pet-focused vacuum cleaner whose profitability had “been challenged” due to “sales price compression/competitive matching” – a reference to Amazon’s practice of matching its competitors’ prices via algorithm.But Newell responded that the guarantee wouldn’t be “necessary”. He said he was confident he could “get Walmart to raise the price back up” so that Amazon could then match it. “This would resolve the margin issues,” he wrote.In response, King, Newell’s counterpart at Amazon, continued to float the possibility of compensation funding, but suggested they not discuss the matter further over email. “This is a delicate conversation for numerous reasons and probably best suited for a phone call/virtual meeting,” she said.In an interview with the Guardian, Newell said he did not recall the particulars of the incident but said the incentives that Amazon had created for brands made raising prices with competitors an obvious solution.“It’s kind of like basic marketing understanding and math,” he said.King did not answer questions from the Guardian about the exchange.Newell noted that Amazon vendor managers would tell him to schedule price increases with Walmart then reach back out to them, so they could “flip” the price back up “on the same day”. Amazon staffers avoided putting such messages in email, he said.“They know exactly what they’re doing,” he said, adding, “They’re really well coached. They wanna get you on the phone.”George Joseph is an investigative reporter for the Guardian. If you currently work for or previously worked for Amazon or an Amazon supplier, reach out to him with tips and insights via text or the encrypted messaging app Signal at 929-486-4865 or via email at george.joseph@theguardian.com.

blocks.html_en: A “modern leather” table lamp, listed online at Walmart, shot up from $24.99 to $39.

sentences.en: A “modern leather” table lamp, listed online at Walmart, shot up from $24.99 to $39.
sentences.zh: 沃尔玛网上出售的一款“现代皮革”台灯，价格从 24.99 美元飙升至 39 美元。

blocks.html_en: The price of an air fryer on a retail site called Newegg went from $84.99 to $149.99.

sentences.en: The price of an air fryer on a retail site called Newegg went from $84.99 to $149.99.
sentences.zh: Newegg 零售网站上空气炸锅的价格从 84.99 美元涨到了 149.99 美元。

blocks.html_en: An electric ice-cream maker was listed at just $17.99 at Amazon and Best Buy. Then it became unavailable at Best Buy, and more than tripled in price to $59.99 at Amazon.

sentences.en: An electric ice-cream maker was listed at just $17.99 at Amazon and Best Buy.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊和百思买上的一款电动冰淇淋机售价仅为 17.99 美元。

sentences.en: Then it became unavailable at Best Buy, and more than tripled in price to $59.99 at Amazon.
sentences.zh: 然后它在百思买 (Best Buy) 上不再销售，在亚马逊 (Amazon) 上价格上涨了两倍多，达到 59.99 美元。

blocks.html_en: The price hikes and the product disappearances were not the blips of supply and demand – they were the results of behind-the-scenes pressure by Amazon, according to internal emails reviewed by the Guardian and court claims against the world’s largest company.

sentences.en: The price hikes and the product disappearances were not the blips of supply and demand – they were the results of behind-the-scenes pressure by Amazon, according to internal emails reviewed by the Guardian and court claims against the world’s largest company.
sentences.zh: 根据《卫报》查看的内部电子邮件和针对这家全球最大公司的法庭索赔，价格上涨和产品消失并不是供需关系的波动，而是亚马逊幕后施压的结果。

blocks.html_en: For years, as many Americans have felt squeezed by the costs of basic consumer goods, Amazon has deployed a variety of techniques that have incentivized suppliers to push for higher product prices on other retailers’ sites, California authorities claim in court.

sentences.en: For years, as many Americans have felt squeezed by the costs of basic consumer goods, Amazon has deployed a variety of techniques that have incentivized suppliers to push for higher product prices on other retailers’ sites, California authorities claim in court.
sentences.zh: 加州当局在法庭上声称，多年来，由于许多美国人感到基本消费品成本的压力，亚马逊采取了各种技术来激励供应商在其他零售商的网站上推高产品价格。

blocks.html_en: In some internal emails, Amazon employees have flagged low product prices on rival retailers’ sites as threats to Amazon’s own profitability, and informed the products’ suppliers that their sales on Amazon.com have been cut – or might soon be cut – going forward.

sentences.en: In some internal emails, Amazon employees have flagged low product prices on rival retailers’ sites as threats to Amazon’s own profitability, and informed the products’ suppliers that their sales on Amazon.com have been cut – or might soon be cut – going forward.
sentences.zh: 在一些内部电子邮件中，亚马逊员工将竞争对手零售商网站上的低产品价格标记为对亚马逊自身盈利能力的威胁，并告知产品供应商，他们在亚马逊网站上的销售额已被削减（或可能很快被削减）。

blocks.html_en: Other emails describe Amazon slashing its product prices to match retail rivals like Walmart and Home Depot, then pushing suppliers to compensate it for the revenue lost due to these lower prices.

sentences.en: Other emails describe Amazon slashing its product prices to match retail rivals like Walmart and Home Depot, then pushing suppliers to compensate it for the revenue lost due to these lower prices.
sentences.zh: 其他电子邮件描述亚马逊大幅削减产品价格，以与沃尔玛和家得宝等零售竞争对手抗衡，然后敦促供应商补偿因价格降低而损失的收入。

blocks.html_en: To escape this financial pressure, some suppliers have responded with a fast and cheap alternative: making sure the prices of their products are higher with Amazon’s rivals, or pulling their products from those rivals altogether, according to internal records cited in litigation.

sentences.en: To escape this financial pressure, some suppliers have responded with a fast and cheap alternative: making sure the prices of their products are higher with Amazon’s rivals, or pulling their products from those rivals altogether, according to internal records cited in litigation.
sentences.zh: 根据诉讼中引用的内部记录，为了摆脱这种财务压力，一些供应商采取了一种快速而廉价的替代方案：确保他们的产品价格比亚马逊的竞争对手更高，或者从这些竞争对手那里完全撤下他们的产品。

blocks.html_en: Over the last decade, Amazon has suppressed the sales of some suppliers’ products or demanded millions of dollars in compensation from them because of the low prices of Amazon’s retail rivals, according to a trove of internal company emails, presentations and notes reviewed by the Guardian, as well as interviews with 15 current or former Amazon supplier representatives, company employees and state and federal law enforcers.

sentences.en: Over the last decade, Amazon has suppressed the sales of some suppliers’ products or demanded millions of dollars in compensation from them because of the low prices of Amazon’s retail rivals, according to a trove of internal company emails, presentations and notes reviewed by the Guardian, as well as interviews with 15 current or former Amazon supplier representatives, company employees and state and federal law enforcers.
sentences.zh: 根据《卫报》查阅的大量公司内部电子邮件、演示文稿和笔记，以及对 15 名现任或前任亚马逊供应商代表、公司员工以及州和联邦执法人员的采访，在过去十年中，由于亚马逊零售竞争对手的低价，亚马逊压制了一些供应商产品的销售，或要求他们支付数百万美元的赔偿。

blocks.html_en: The mass of internal Amazon records were obtained from Amazon by California’s attorney general as part of a lawsuit that alleges the company has engaged in widespread price fixing, which is defined as arrangements among companies that seek to raise or otherwise control the prices of products or services.

sentences.en: The mass of internal Amazon records were obtained from Amazon by California’s attorney general as part of a lawsuit that alleges the company has engaged in widespread price fixing, which is defined as arrangements among companies that seek to raise or otherwise control the prices of products or services.
sentences.zh: 加利福尼亚州总检察长在一项诉讼中从亚马逊获得了亚马逊的大量内部记录，该诉讼指控该公司参与了广泛的价格操纵行为，这种行为被定义为公司之间寻求提高或以其他方式控制产品或服务价格的安排。

blocks.html_en: The Guardian examined those records, as well as hundreds of pages of deposition testimony of current and former employees of Amazon and supplier companies. Most of the documents and depositions – which were partially unsealed in recent months – have never been publicly reported on.

sentences.en: The Guardian examined those records, as well as hundreds of pages of deposition testimony of current and former employees of Amazon and supplier companies.
sentences.zh: 《卫报》审查了这些记录，以及亚马逊和供应商公司现任和前任员工的数百页证词。

sentences.en: Most of the documents and depositions – which were partially unsealed in recent months – have never been publicly reported on.
sentences.zh: 大多数文件和证词——近几个月部分被公开——从未被公开报道过。

blocks.html_en: The California attorney general, Rob Bonta, alleges that Amazon’s tactics have coerced suppliers into raising the prices of their products with its rivals.

sentences.en: The California attorney general, Rob Bonta, alleges that Amazon’s tactics have coerced suppliers into raising the prices of their products with its rivals.
sentences.zh: 加州总检察长罗布·邦塔 (Rob Bonta) 声称，亚马逊的策略迫使供应商提高其竞争对手产品的价格。

blocks.html_en: In the case of the ice-cream maker, Bonta’s office points to emails that say Amazon temporarily took “down” the supplier’s inventory off its platform – a move that prompted its manufacturer, a firm called Maxi-Matic, to scramble to pull its inventory from Best Buy, eliminating Amazon’s price competition from the electronics retailer.

sentences.en: In the case of the ice-cream maker, Bonta’s office points to emails that say Amazon temporarily took “down” the supplier’s inventory off its platform – a move that prompted its manufacturer, a firm called Maxi-Matic, to scramble to pull its inventory from Best Buy, eliminating Amazon’s price competition from the electronics retailer.
sentences.zh: 就这家冰淇淋制造商而言，Bonta 办公室指出，电子邮件称亚马逊暂时从其平台上“下架”了该供应商的库存，此举促使其制造商 Maxi-Matic 争相从百思买撤下库存，从而消除了亚马逊与这家电子零售商的价格竞争。

blocks.html_en: By the end of that day, Amazon had put Maxi-Matic’s product “back live” on Amazon.com and more than tripled its price, the records show.

sentences.en: By the end of that day, Amazon had put Maxi-Matic’s product “back live” on Amazon.com and more than tripled its price, the records show.
sentences.zh: 记录显示，到当天结束时，亚马逊已将 Maxi-Matic 的产品“重新上线”在 Amazon.com 上，并将其价格提高了两倍多。

blocks.html_en: In the case of the air fryer, Amazon “suppressed” the product’s sales on its platform and told its supplier that it would stop ordering several of its products for Amazon.com – unless the supplier agreed to reimburse Amazon for revenues the online retailer had lost due to its practice of copying competitors’ lower prices, according to emails revealed through the attorney general’s lawsuit.

sentences.en: In the case of the air fryer, Amazon “suppressed” the product’s sales on its platform and told its supplier that it would stop ordering several of its products for Amazon.com – unless the supplier agreed to reimburse Amazon for revenues the online retailer had lost due to its practice of copying competitors’ lower prices, according to emails revealed through the attorney general’s lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 在空气炸锅一案中，根据总检察长诉讼中透露的电子邮件，亚马逊“压制”了该产品在其平台上的销售，并告诉其供应商，它将停止为亚马逊网站订购其多种产品，除非供应商同意向亚马逊赔偿这家在线零售商因抄袭竞争对手低价行为而损失的收入。

blocks.html_en: This spurred the supplier, a kitchen appliance firm called Chefman, to move to secure a price increase for the air fryer at Target and an online retailer called Newegg as it hashed out an agreement to reimburse Amazon $100,000 on top of the $400,000 it had already given back, according to the emails.

sentences.en: This spurred the supplier, a kitchen appliance firm called Chefman, to move to secure a price increase for the air fryer at Target and an online retailer called Newegg as it hashed out an agreement to reimburse Amazon $100,000 on top of the $400,000 it had already given back, according to the emails.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件显示，这促使供应商（一家名为 Chefman 的厨房电器公司）采取行动，以确保塔吉特（Target）和一家名为 Newegg 的在线零售商的空气炸锅涨价，因为该供应商制定了一项协议，在已返还亚马逊 40 万美元的基础上，再向亚马逊偿还 10 万美元。

blocks.html_en: Asked about the air fryer’s price jump, Mark Friedman, Chefman’s senior vice-president of global sales, told the Guardian: “I can’t talk to you because I still do business with Amazon and I don’t want to bite the hand that feeds me.”

sentences.en: Asked about the air fryer’s price jump, Mark Friedman, Chefman’s senior vice-president of global sales, told the Guardian: “I can’t talk to you because I still do business with Amazon and I don’t want to bite the hand that feeds me.”
sentences.zh: 当被问及空气炸锅的价格上涨时，Chefman 全球销售高级副总裁马克·弗里德曼 (Mark Friedman) 告诉《卫报》：“我无法与你交谈，因为我仍然与亚马逊做生意，我不想咬住养活我的手。”

blocks.html_en: Amazon’s written responses to the Guardian did not respond to questions about these specific incidents. The company accuses the California attorney general of “distorting a handful of emails” out of “nearly one million vendor communications” to “suggest a far-reaching conspiracy among unspecified thousands of vendors and retailers”.

sentences.en: Amazon’s written responses to the Guardian did not respond to questions about these specific incidents.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊给《卫报》的书面回复没有回应有关这些具体事件的问题。

sentences.en: The company accuses the California attorney general of “distorting a handful of emails” out of “nearly one million vendor communications” to “suggest a far-reaching conspiracy among unspecified thousands of vendors and retailers”.
sentences.zh: 该公司指责加州总检察长从“近一百万份供应商通信”中“歪曲了几封电子邮件”，以“暗示数千名供应商和零售商之间存在影响深远的阴谋”。

blocks.html_en: Amazon says it works hard to protect customers from “paying too much” and to negotiate economic terms that allow it “to offer products at the competitive prices” that “customers expect from Amazon”. It also says the California attorney general’s suit is “asking the court to prevent Amazon from negotiating with its vendors to lower prices, likely leading to higher prices for Amazon’s customers”.

sentences.en: Amazon says it works hard to protect customers from “paying too much” and to negotiate economic terms that allow it “to offer products at the competitive prices” that “customers expect from Amazon”.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊表示，它努力保护客户免于“支付过高费用”，并协商经济条款，使其能够“以具有竞争力的价格提供产品”，从而满足“客户对亚马逊的期望”。

sentences.en: It also says the California attorney general’s suit is “asking the court to prevent Amazon from negotiating with its vendors to lower prices, likely leading to higher prices for Amazon’s customers”.
sentences.zh: 它还表示，加州总检察长的诉讼“要求法院阻止亚马逊与其供应商谈判降低价格，这可能导致亚马逊客户的价格上涨”。

blocks.html_en: The incident involving the price hike for the leather table lamp, for example, was part of a broader exchange about dozens of products on Amazon.com sourced from a New Jersey based supplier called All The Rages.

sentences.en: The incident involving the price hike for the leather table lamp, for example, was part of a broader exchange about dozens of products on Amazon.com sourced from a New Jersey based supplier called All The Rages.
sentences.zh: 例如，涉及皮革台灯涨价的事件是亚马逊网站上数十种产品的更广泛交易的一部分，这些产品均来自新泽西州一家名为 All The Rages 的供应商。

blocks.html_en: The email exchange shows that Amazon negotiated for cost decreases for many of the supplier’s other products, ensuring lower prices for consumers on Amazon.com.

sentences.en: The email exchange shows that Amazon negotiated for cost decreases for many of the supplier’s other products, ensuring lower prices for consumers on Amazon.com.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件往来显示，亚马逊就供应商的许多其他产品的成本降低进行了谈判，确保亚马逊网站上的消费者获得更低的价格。

blocks.html_en: But the messages also refer to raising the prices of other items sold by Amazon’s competitors.

sentences.en: But the messages also refer to raising the prices of other items sold by Amazon’s competitors.
sentences.zh: 但这些消息还提到提高亚马逊竞争对手销售的其他商品的价格。

blocks.html_en: After Amazon reached out, an All The Rages employee named Joseph Martin replied that his company had “contacted” various unnamed retailers “to fix the retail” and had seen an increase in retail prices “for a lot of the items already”.

sentences.en: After Amazon reached out, an All The Rages employee named Joseph Martin replied that his company had “contacted” various unnamed retailers “to fix the retail” and had seen an increase in retail prices “for a lot of the items already”.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊联系后，一位名叫约瑟夫·马丁（Joseph Martin）的 All The Rages 员工回复称，他的公司已“联系”了多家未透露姓名的零售商“以修复零售问题”，并发现“许多商品的零售价格已经上涨”。

blocks.html_en: Two days later, Martin told Amazon that the lamp price at Walmart had increased from $24.99 to $39. “We should be good now,” he said.

sentences.en: Two days later, Martin told Amazon that the lamp price at Walmart had increased from $24.99 to $39.
sentences.zh: 两天后，马丁告诉亚马逊，沃尔玛的灯价格从 24.99 美元涨到了 39 美元。

sentences.en: “We should be good now,” he said.
sentences.zh: “我们现在应该很好，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Martin told the Guardian that the lamp’s price change “reflected the correction of a pricing error”.

sentences.en: Martin told the Guardian that the lamp’s price change “reflected the correction of a pricing error”.
sentences.zh: 马丁告诉卫报，灯的价格变化“反映了定价错误的纠正”。

blocks.html_en: “Any decision regarding retail pricing was made by Walmart and Amazon, so you would have to ask them about their pricing decisions,” said Martin, who added that his company believes the California attorney general’s claims “are without merit”.

sentences.en: “Any decision regarding retail pricing was made by Walmart and Amazon, so you would have to ask them about their pricing decisions,” said Martin, who added that his company believes the California attorney general’s claims “are without merit”.
sentences.zh: 马丁说：“任何有关零售定价的决定都是由沃尔玛和亚马逊做出的，因此你必须询问他们的定价决定。”他补充说，他的公司认为加州总检察长的说法“没有根据”。

blocks.html_en: Amazon and Walmart declined to provide statements about this specific incident.

sentences.en: Amazon and Walmart declined to provide statements about this specific incident.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊和沃尔玛拒绝就这一具体事件发表声明。

blocks.html_en: ‘It seemed like he knew what was happening’

sentences.en: ‘It seemed like he knew what was happening’
sentences.zh: “他似乎知道发生了什么事”

blocks.html_en: While such tactics can generate savings for Amazon consumers, they can also raise prices for consumers shopping elsewhere, an effect that company employees on the ground were well aware of, according to supplier representatives as well as former Amazon employees who spoke to the Guardian on the condition of anonymity.

sentences.en: While such tactics can generate savings for Amazon consumers, they can also raise prices for consumers shopping elsewhere, an effect that company employees on the ground were well aware of, according to supplier representatives as well as former Amazon employees who spoke to the Guardian on the condition of anonymity.
sentences.zh: 供应商代表以及不愿透露姓名的亚马逊前员工表示，虽然这种策略可以为亚马逊消费者节省开支，但也可能会提高消费者在其他地方购物的价格，公司当地员工都清楚这种影响。

blocks.html_en: One former vendor manager, who worked at Amazon from 2022 to 2024 before getting laid off, said that on phone calls with suppliers he tried to be upfront about the fact that raising prices with Amazon’s competitors could help them avoid demands that they compensate Amazon for lower-than-anticipated profit margins.

sentences.en: One former vendor manager, who worked at Amazon from 2022 to 2024 before getting laid off, said that on phone calls with suppliers he tried to be upfront about the fact that raising prices with Amazon’s competitors could help them avoid demands that they compensate Amazon for lower-than-anticipated profit margins.
sentences.zh: 一位在 2022 年至 2024 年被解雇前在亚马逊工作的前供应商经理表示，在与供应商的电话中，他试图坦白这样一个事实：与亚马逊的竞争对手一起提高价格可以帮助他们避免要求他们赔偿亚马逊低于预期的利润率。

blocks.html_en: “Hey, if you can make sure this product doesn’t continue to be sold at this lower price point, then we don’t have to keep revisiting this discussion about margin,” the former Amazon vendor manager recalled telling suppliers.

sentences.en: “Hey, if you can make sure this product doesn’t continue to be sold at this lower price point, then we don’t have to keep revisiting this discussion about margin,” the former Amazon vendor manager recalled telling suppliers.
sentences.zh: “嘿，如果你能确保这款产品不会继续以这么低的价格出售，那么我们就不必继续重新审视有关利润的讨论，”这位前亚马逊供应商经理回忆道，他曾这样告诉供应商。

blocks.html_en: A former Amazon “customer success” manager said that Amazon staff had “very black-and-white conversations” with suppliers about the prospect of raising prices with Amazon’s competitors.

sentences.en: A former Amazon “customer success” manager said that Amazon staff had “very black-and-white conversations” with suppliers about the prospect of raising prices with Amazon’s competitors.
sentences.zh: 一位前亚马逊“客户成功”经理表示，亚马逊员工与供应商就与亚马逊竞争对手提价的前景进行了“非常黑白分明的对话”。

blocks.html_en: “They’d say, ‘You need to go to Target. You need to go talk to Walmart to get them to raise their price,’” he recalled.

sentences.en: “They’d say, ‘You need to go to Target.
sentences.zh: “他们会说，‘你需要去塔吉特百货。

sentences.en: You need to go talk to Walmart to get them to raise their price,’” he recalled.
sentences.zh: 你需要去和沃尔玛谈谈，让他们提高价格，”他回忆道。

sentences.en: An Amazon Prime delivery van sits parked near a Walmart store on 3 September 2020 in Richmond, California.
sentences.zh: 2020 年 9 月 3 日，加利福尼亚州里士满，一辆亚马逊 Prime 送货车停在沃尔玛商店附近。

sentences.en: Photograph: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
sentences.zh: 照片：贾斯汀·沙利文/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: The former customer success manager told the Guardian that higher-ups instructed Amazon employees to have certain conversations with suppliers over the phone in order to avoid a digital trail of potential price-fixing allegations.

sentences.en: The former customer success manager told the Guardian that higher-ups instructed Amazon employees to have certain conversations with suppliers over the phone in order to avoid a digital trail of potential price-fixing allegations.
sentences.zh: 这位前客户成功经理告诉《卫报》，高层指示亚马逊员工通过电话与供应商进行某些对话，以避免潜在的价格操纵指控的数字线索。

blocks.html_en: “The only rule was, ‘Do not have this in writing’,” he recalled.

sentences.en: “The only rule was, ‘Do not have this in writing’,” he recalled.
sentences.zh: “唯一的规则是，‘不要将其书面化’，”他回忆道。

blocks.html_en: The former employees’ accounts echo several Amazon emails and presentations relied on in court by California’s attorney general. One from 2022 reminds staff to “not use email” for certain conversations with suppliers. Another email from 2020 reminds staff to “keep in mind it is often best to have these conversations over the phone”.

sentences.en: The former employees’ accounts echo several Amazon emails and presentations relied on in court by California’s attorney general.
sentences.zh: 这些前员工的账户与加州总检察长在法庭上引用的几封亚马逊电子邮件和演示文稿相呼应。

sentences.en: One from 2022 reminds staff to “not use email” for certain conversations with suppliers.
sentences.zh: 2022 年的一项政策提醒员工“不要使用电子邮件”与供应商进行某些对话。

sentences.en: Another email from 2020 reminds staff to “keep in mind it is often best to have these conversations over the phone”.
sentences.zh: 2020 年的另一封电子邮件提醒员工“记住，通常最好通过电话进行这些对话”。

blocks.html_en: Amazon did not directly respond to questions about these sources’ statements and those presentations cited in court by the attorney general. But in legal filings, the company has pointed to other instructions it disseminated to staff barring them from making agreements with vendors or competitors on pricing.

sentences.en: Amazon did not directly respond to questions about these sources’ statements and those presentations cited in court by the attorney general.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊没有直接回应有关这些消息来源的声明以及总检察长在法庭上引用的陈述的问题。

sentences.en: But in legal filings, the company has pointed to other instructions it disseminated to staff barring them from making agreements with vendors or competitors on pricing.
sentences.zh: 但在法律文件中，该公司指出，它向员工发出了其他指示，禁止他们与供应商或竞争对手就定价达成协议。

blocks.html_en: Ryan Turano, chief technology officer of a fertilizer company called AgroThrive, told the Guardian that when Amazon’s profitability was threatened due to its price matching, an Amazon vendor manager would reach out to him and sometimes list off businesses like Walmart or Home Depot for him to contact.

sentences.en: Ryan Turano, chief technology officer of a fertilizer company called AgroThrive, told the Guardian that when Amazon’s profitability was threatened due to its price matching, an Amazon vendor manager would reach out to him and sometimes list off businesses like Walmart or Home Depot for him to contact.
sentences.zh: 化肥公司 AgroThrive 的首席技术官瑞安·图拉诺 (Ryan Turano) 告诉《卫报》，当亚马逊的盈利能力因价格匹配而受到威胁时，亚马逊的供应商经理会联系他，有时会列出沃尔玛或家得宝等企业供他联系。

blocks.html_en: “He’d go, ‘Home Depot is probably causing the problem, talk to your vendor manager there to see what you could do,’” recalled Turano. “It seemed like he knew what was happening.”

sentences.en: “He’d go, ‘Home Depot is probably causing the problem, talk to your vendor manager there to see what you could do,’” recalled Turano.
sentences.zh: “他会说，‘问题可能是家得宝造成的，请与那里的供应商经理谈谈，看看你能做什么，’”图拉诺回忆道。

sentences.en: “It seemed like he knew what was happening.”
sentences.zh: “看来他知道发生了什么事。”

blocks.html_en: In one internal email, that Amazon manager asked Turano for an “update on the price match issue we discussed”. Turano told him that he had “just got out of a meeting with the Home Depot manager,” who had “agreed to raise the prices this time”.

sentences.en: In one internal email, that Amazon manager asked Turano for an “update on the price match issue we discussed”.
sentences.zh: 在一封内部电子邮件中，亚马逊经理要求图拉诺提供“我们讨论的价格匹配问题的最新情况”。

sentences.en: Turano told him that he had “just got out of a meeting with the Home Depot manager,” who had “agreed to raise the prices this time”.
sentences.zh: 图拉诺告诉他，他“刚刚与家得宝经理开完会”，后者“同意这次提高价格”。

blocks.html_en: Home Depot declined to comment on the email.

sentences.en: Home Depot declined to comment on the email.
sentences.zh: 家得宝拒绝对这封电子邮件发表评论。

blocks.html_en: Turano blamed Amazon for the pressure that resulted in price increases like the one he negotiated for with the home improvement retailer.

sentences.en: Turano blamed Amazon for the pressure that resulted in price increases like the one he negotiated for with the home improvement retailer.
sentences.zh: 图拉诺指责亚马逊施加压力，导致价格上涨，就像他与家居装修零售商谈判达成的那样。

blocks.html_en: “We were at their mercy. Because we didn’t have the resources to push back,” Turano said.

sentences.en: “We were at their mercy.
sentences.zh: “我们任由他们摆布。

sentences.en: Because we didn’t have the resources to push back,” Turano said.
sentences.zh: 因为我们没有资源来反击，”图拉诺说。

blocks.html_en: “It was just a nightmare. It didn’t feel good,” he added. “It’s almost understandable. Because they’re so big they can do whatever they want.”

sentences.en: “It was just a nightmare.
sentences.zh: “这只是一场噩梦。

sentences.en: It didn’t feel good,” he added.
sentences.zh: 感觉不太好，”他补充道。

sentences.en: “It’s almost understandable.
sentences.zh: “这几乎是可以理解的。

sentences.en: Because they’re so big they can do whatever they want.”
sentences.zh: 因为他们太大了，他们可以做任何他们想做的事。”

blocks.html_en: Amazon – which recently surpassed Walmart as the world’s biggest company by revenue – is currently facing multiple court cases alleging price-fixing.

sentences.en: Amazon – which recently surpassed Walmart as the world’s biggest company by revenue – is currently facing multiple court cases alleging price-fixing.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊最近超越沃尔玛成为全球收入最大的公司，目前正面临多起涉嫌操纵价格的法庭案件。

blocks.html_en: Price-fixing lawsuits against Amazon being pursued by the California attorney general and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) are both slated to go to trial in early 2027. The company is also battling similar claims in a private class action lawsuit in federal court in Seattle and a legal action pursued by Washington DC’s attorney general, which was initially dismissed but revived on appeal.

sentences.en: Price-fixing lawsuits against Amazon being pursued by the California attorney general and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) are both slated to go to trial in early 2027.
sentences.zh: 加州总检察长和联邦贸易委员会 (FTC) 对亚马逊提起的价格垄断诉讼预计将于 2027 年初进行审判。

sentences.en: The company is also battling similar claims in a private class action lawsuit in federal court in Seattle and a legal action pursued by Washington DC’s attorney general, which was initially dismissed but revived on appeal.
sentences.zh: 该公司还在西雅图联邦法院提起的私人集体诉讼中，以及华盛顿特区总检察长提起的法律诉讼中，与类似的主张作斗争，该诉讼最初被驳回，但在上诉后又恢复了。

blocks.html_en: Amazon denies the claims in the various court actions and says its practices encourage lower prices, incentivize competition and create greater product selection for consumers.

sentences.en: Amazon denies the claims in the various court actions and says its practices encourage lower prices, incentivize competition and create greater product selection for consumers.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊否认了各种法庭诉讼中的指控，并表示其做法鼓励降低价格、激励竞争并为消费者创造更多产品选择。

blocks.html_en: In the California case, Amazon told the Guardian that the price fixing theory asserted by the attorney general is “legally untenable, with the state offering no evidence of any communication between Amazon and any other retailer, much less an agreement among them to fix prices. The practices California challenges are common in the retail industry and part of the pro-competitive give-and-take between Amazon and its vendors.”

sentences.en: In the California case, Amazon told the Guardian that the price fixing theory asserted by the attorney general is “legally untenable, with the state offering no evidence of any communication between Amazon and any other retailer, much less an agreement among them to fix prices.
sentences.zh: 在加州案件中，亚马逊告诉《卫报》，总检察长所主张的定价理论“在法律上是站不住脚的，因为该州没有提供亚马逊与任何其他零售商之间任何沟通的证据，更不用说他们之间达成了定价协议。”

sentences.en: The practices California challenges are common in the retail industry and part of the pro-competitive give-and-take between Amazon and its vendors.”
sentences.zh: 加州挑战的做法在零售行业很常见，也是亚马逊与其供应商之间有利于竞争的妥协的一部分。”

blocks.html_en: In 2022 Amazon agreed to pay $2.25m to resolve claims by Washington state’s attorney general that it forced some merchants to raise prices for products they sold through the company’s “Sold by Amazon” plan. The company made no admission of liability and said it strongly believed the program was legal.

sentences.en: In 2022 Amazon agreed to pay $2.25m to resolve claims by Washington state’s attorney general that it forced some merchants to raise prices for products they sold through the company’s “Sold by Amazon” plan.
sentences.zh: 2022 年，亚马逊同意支付 225 万美元，以解决华盛顿州总检察长提出的指控，称亚马逊迫使一些商家提高通过该公司的“亚马逊销售”计划销售的产品的价格。

sentences.en: The company made no admission of liability and said it strongly believed the program was legal.
sentences.zh: 该公司没有承认承担责任，并表示坚信该计划是合法的。

blocks.html_en: The power of algorithms

sentences.en: The power of algorithms
sentences.zh: 算法的力量

blocks.html_en: Amazon has long prided itself on offering Americans the lowest prices online.

sentences.en: Amazon has long prided itself on offering Americans the lowest prices online.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊长期以来一直以向美国人提供最低的在线价格而自豪。

blocks.html_en: The company points to studies showing that over the last nine years, Amazon has consistently offered consumers the lowest prices among major American retailers and academic research suggesting that the competition between online and traditional retailers has driven down product prices and might have actually tamped down on inflation in years past. According to a 2025 study from the analytics firm Profitero+, Amazon’s prices were on average 14% lower than its leading competitors.

sentences.en: The company points to studies showing that over the last nine years, Amazon has consistently offered consumers the lowest prices among major American retailers and academic research suggesting that the competition between online and traditional retailers has driven down product prices and might have actually tamped down on inflation in years past.
sentences.zh: 该公司指出，研究表明，在过去的九年里，亚马逊一直向消费者提供美国主要零售商中最低的价格，学术研究表明，在线零售商和传统零售商之间的竞争压低了产品价格，并可能在过去几年实际上抑制了通货膨胀。

sentences.en: According to a 2025 study from the analytics firm Profitero+, Amazon’s prices were on average 14% lower than its leading competitors.
sentences.zh: 根据分析公司 Profitero+ 2025 年的一项研究，亚马逊的价格比其主要竞争对手平均低 14%。

blocks.html_en: But the FTC and California lawsuits allege the company’s vaunted price floor is a mirage, obscuring its behind-the-scenes efforts to drive up its competitors’ prices.

sentences.en: But the FTC and California lawsuits allege the company’s vaunted price floor is a mirage, obscuring its behind-the-scenes efforts to drive up its competitors’ prices.
sentences.zh: 但联邦贸易委员会和加州的诉讼称，该公司所吹嘘的价格下限只是一个海市蜃楼，掩盖了其抬高竞争对手价格的幕后努力。

blocks.html_en: Throughout the 2010s, Amazon systematically mapped the prices of its main retail rivals across the internet, according to testimony of current and former Amazon employees in depositions conducted by the FTC and California authorities.

sentences.en: Throughout the 2010s, Amazon systematically mapped the prices of its main retail rivals across the internet, according to testimony of current and former Amazon employees in depositions conducted by the FTC and California authorities.
sentences.zh: 根据亚马逊现任和前任员工在联邦贸易委员会和加州当局的证词中的证词，在整个 2010 年代，亚马逊系统地绘制了互联网上主要零售竞争对手的价格图。

blocks.html_en: In 2011, Amazon had a team of about 200 to 250 people using a hybrid mix of manual and automated methods to surveil competitors’ prices, Karthik Mitta, a former Amazon director, testified last year in a deposition in the FTC case. By 2019, Mitta testified, that team had grown to nearly 2,000 people, enabling the company to use web crawlers to collect pricing information and monitor tens of thousands of competitor sites.

sentences.en: In 2011, Amazon had a team of about 200 to 250 people using a hybrid mix of manual and automated methods to surveil competitors’ prices, Karthik Mitta, a former Amazon director, testified last year in a deposition in the FTC case.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊前董事 Karthik Mitta 去年在 FTC 案件的证词中作证称，2011 年，亚马逊拥有一支约 200 至 250 人的团队，使用手动和自动混合方法来监控竞争对手的价格。

sentences.en: By 2019, Mitta testified, that team had grown to nearly 2,000 people, enabling the company to use web crawlers to collect pricing information and monitor tens of thousands of competitor sites.
sentences.zh: Mitta 作证说，到 2019 年，该团队已发展到近 2,000 人，使公司能够使用网络爬虫来收集定价信息并监控数以万计的竞争对手网站。

blocks.html_en: Harnessing this knowledge of pricing data across the internet, Amazon adopted what one executive called a “game theory” approach to pricing which could help it evade the costs of a “perfectly competitive market”, according to the FTC’s lawsuit.

sentences.en: Harnessing this knowledge of pricing data across the internet, Amazon adopted what one executive called a “game theory” approach to pricing which could help it evade the costs of a “perfectly competitive market”, according to the FTC’s lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 根据联邦贸易委员会的诉讼，亚马逊利用互联网上的定价数据知识，采用了一位高管所说的“博弈论”定价方法，这可以帮助它规避“完全竞争市场”的成本。

blocks.html_en: Amazon developed an algorithm that rapidly copied rivals’ price changes “to the penny”, according to the FTC. If retail rivals raised or lowered its price, the FTC claimed, Amazon would do the same.

sentences.en: Amazon developed an algorithm that rapidly copied rivals’ price changes “to the penny”, according to the FTC.
sentences.zh: 据联邦贸易委员会称，亚马逊开发了一种算法，可以快速复制竞争对手的价格变化，“精确到一分钱”。

sentences.en: If retail rivals raised or lowered its price, the FTC claimed, Amazon would do the same.
sentences.zh: 联邦贸易委员会声称，如果零售竞争对手提高或降低其价格，亚马逊也会采取同样的做法。

sentences.en: An associate updates the price on a sale item at a Walmart store on Black Friday in Columbus, Ohio, on 28 November 2025.
sentences.zh: 2025 年 11 月 28 日，黑色星期五，俄亥俄州哥伦布市一家沃尔玛商店的一名员工更新了促销商品的价格。

sentences.en: Photograph: Brian Kaiser/Bloomberg/Getty Images
sentences.zh: 照片：布莱恩·凯泽/彭博社/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: The company did not respond directly to questions regarding allegations about its algorithm, but pointed the Guardian to academic literature and industry reports noting how common it is for retailers to track prices and for suppliers to try to enforce minimum prices for their products.

sentences.en: The company did not respond directly to questions regarding allegations about its algorithm, but pointed the Guardian to academic literature and industry reports noting how common it is for retailers to track prices and for suppliers to try to enforce minimum prices for their products.
sentences.zh: 该公司没有直接回应有关其算法指控的问题，但向《卫报》指出了学术文献和行业报告，指出零售商追踪价格和供应商试图强制执行其产品最低价格的情况是多么普遍。

blocks.html_en: Arjun Narayan, a former general manager for Amazon’s vendor program, told the Guardian that the company’s price-following tactics were designed to ensure that the company continued to fulfill founder Jeff Bezos’s original mission: that it strive to help customers find “anything they might want to buy online” at “the lowest possible prices”.

sentences.en: Arjun Narayan, a former general manager for Amazon’s vendor program, told the Guardian that the company’s price-following tactics were designed to ensure that the company continued to fulfill founder Jeff Bezos’s original mission: that it strive to help customers find “anything they might want to buy online” at “the lowest possible prices”.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊供应商计划前总经理阿琼·纳拉扬 (Arjun Narayan) 告诉《卫报》，该公司的价格跟踪策略旨在确保该公司继续履行创始人杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 最初的使命：努力帮助客户以“尽可能低的价格”找到“他们可能想在网上购买的任何东西”。

blocks.html_en: “If I figure out that you’re actually selling at a lower price elsewhere, the very promise is defeated, right?” noted Narayan, who now advises brands with his own firm, SalesDuo.

sentences.en: “If I figure out that you’re actually selling at a lower price elsewhere, the very promise is defeated, right?”
sentences.zh: “如果我发现你实际上在其他地方以更低的价格出售，那么这个承诺就落空了，对吗？”

sentences.en: noted Narayan, who now advises brands with his own firm, SalesDuo.
sentences.zh: 纳拉扬 (Narayan) 说道，他现在在自己的公司 SalesDuo 中为品牌提供建议。

blocks.html_en: The FTC argues the algorithm sought to instill another lesson among Amazon’s competitors: they could not compete with Amazon by offering lower prices, so with no better options left, they may as well raise their prices – increases that Amazon’s algorithm would then happily follow.

sentences.en: The FTC argues the algorithm sought to instill another lesson among Amazon’s competitors: they could not compete with Amazon by offering lower prices, so with no better options left, they may as well raise their prices – increases that Amazon’s algorithm would then happily follow.
sentences.zh: 联邦贸易委员会认为，该算法试图向亚马逊的竞争对手灌输另一个教训：他们无法通过提供更低的价格与亚马逊竞争，因此，在没有更好的选择的情况下，他们可能会提高价格——亚马逊的算法将乐意遵循这种价格上涨。

blocks.html_en: ‘We will discontinue from your problematic competition’

sentences.en: ‘We will discontinue from your problematic competition’
sentences.zh: “我们将停止与你们有问题的竞争”

blocks.html_en: In 2020, three days before Black Friday, Amazon informed Armen Living, a California based furniture supplier, that it “had to suppress and remove” one of its office chairs from its platform, according to emails released in the California lawsuit.

sentences.en: In 2020, three days before Black Friday, Amazon informed Armen Living, a California based furniture supplier, that it “had to suppress and remove” one of its office chairs from its platform, according to emails released in the California lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 根据加州诉讼中公布的电子邮件，2020 年，即黑色星期五前三天，亚马逊通知加州家具供应商 Armen Living，“必须从其平台上压制并删除”其一张办公椅。

blocks.html_en: Amazon had bought the chair, a minimalist curvy unit with a walnut wood exterior and a chrome-plated steel base, for $79 a pop, and had planned to sell them on its site for $116.45.

sentences.en: Amazon had bought the chair, a minimalist curvy unit with a walnut wood exterior and a chrome-plated steel base, for $79 a pop, and had planned to sell them on its site for $116.45.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊以每把 79 美元的价格购买了这款椅子，这是一款极简主义的曲线单元，外层为胡桃木，底座为镀铬钢，并计划在其网站上以 116.45 美元的价格出售。

blocks.html_en: But another retailer in the market happened to be selling the same chair for just $58.62, and Amazon had matched that price, which was so low that Amazon was losing money on every sale.

sentences.en: But another retailer in the market happened to be selling the same chair for just $58.62, and Amazon had matched that price, which was so low that Amazon was losing money on every sale.
sentences.zh: 但市场上的另一家零售商恰好以 58.62 美元的价格出售同一款椅子，而亚马逊也匹配了这个价格，这个价格太低了，以至于亚马逊每次销售都赔钱。

blocks.html_en: Following the price reduction, Amazon made clear to Armen Living that if it wanted to have the chair “back” up on “the website soon”, it was up to the furniture brand to do something about the financial squeeze caused by Amazon’s own programmatic price matching.

sentences.en: Following the price reduction, Amazon made clear to Armen Living that if it wanted to have the chair “back” up on “the website soon”, it was up to the furniture brand to do something about the financial squeeze caused by Amazon’s own programmatic price matching.
sentences.zh: 降价后，亚马逊向 Armen Living 明确表示，如果它想让这把椅子“尽快”重新出现在“网站上”，那么该家具品牌就需要采取措施来应对亚马逊自己的程序化价格匹配造成的财务紧张。

blocks.html_en: In an email, Paige Nguyen, an Amazon vendor manager, laid out three options that the furniture company could choose from to “help address” the issue:

sentences.en: In an email, Paige Nguyen, an Amazon vendor manager, laid out three options that the furniture company could choose from to “help address” the issue:
sentences.zh: 亚马逊供应商经理 Paige Nguyen 在一封电子邮件中列出了家具公司可以选择的三个选项来“帮助解决”该问题：

blocks.html_en: It could compensate Amazon for the revenue loss – paying Amazon $57.83 for every chair sold on the site.

sentences.en: It could compensate Amazon for the revenue loss – paying Amazon $57.83 for every chair sold on the site.
sentences.zh: 它可以补偿亚马逊的收入损失——为网站上售出的每把椅子向亚马逊支付 57.83 美元。

blocks.html_en: It could slash the amount it was selling the chairs to Amazon, a future giveaway to the retail colossus.

sentences.en: It could slash the amount it was selling the chairs to Amazon, a future giveaway to the retail colossus.
sentences.zh: 它可能会大幅削减向亚马逊销售椅子的数量，这是未来向这家零售巨头的赠品。

blocks.html_en: Or – likely the least costly option – it could “manage” the “other channels” where it sold the chair, a euphemism pointing to the Amazon competitors who were selling the chair for less, according to the California lawsuit.

sentences.en: Or – likely the least costly option – it could “manage” the “other channels” where it sold the chair, a euphemism pointing to the Amazon competitors who were selling the chair for less, according to the California lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 或者——这可能是成本最低的选择——它可以“管理”销售椅子的“其他渠道”，根据加州的诉讼，这是一种委婉的说法，指的是亚马逊的竞争对手以更低的价格出售椅子。

blocks.html_en: Nguyen didn’t elaborate on what managing these other “channels” meant, sticking to the kind of language that Amazon uses when training staff on how to talk with suppliers. The California attorney general alleges terms like “channel optimization” are a “naming gloss” for Amazon’s push to get suppliers to enforce minimum prices with or pull inventory from Amazon’s competitors.

sentences.en: Nguyen didn’t elaborate on what managing these other “channels” meant, sticking to the kind of language that Amazon uses when training staff on how to talk with suppliers.
sentences.zh: Nguyen 没有详细说明管理这些其他“渠道”的含义，他坚持使用亚马逊在培训员工如何与供应商交谈时使用的语言。

sentences.en: The California attorney general alleges terms like “channel optimization” are a “naming gloss” for Amazon’s push to get suppliers to enforce minimum prices with or pull inventory from Amazon’s competitors.
sentences.zh: 加州总检察长声称，“渠道优化”等术语是亚马逊推动供应商强制执行最低价格或从亚马逊竞争对手那里撤走库存的“名称修饰”。

blocks.html_en: Rather than paying compensation or offering a cost decrease to Amazon, Armen Living made clear it understood just how it could manage its channels to appease the online retailer.

sentences.en: Rather than paying compensation or offering a cost decrease to Amazon, Armen Living made clear it understood just how it could manage its channels to appease the online retailer.
sentences.zh: Armen Living 没有向亚马逊支付赔偿或降低成本，而是明确表示它了解如何管理其渠道来安抚这家在线零售商。

blocks.html_en: The company promised it would “look further into the potential offenders” in the market offering low prices and pull its inventory from those Amazon competitors if need be.

sentences.en: The company promised it would “look further into the potential offenders” in the market offering low prices and pull its inventory from those Amazon competitors if need be.
sentences.zh: 该公司承诺，将“进一步调查市场上提供低价产品的潜在违规者”，并在必要时从亚马逊竞争对手那里撤回库存。

blocks.html_en: “Amazon is always our top priority,” noted an Armen Living marketing analyst: “… If the problematic retail does not fix by the end of the week, we will discontinue from your problematic competition to ensure that Amazon can return to a healthy state with these items.”

sentences.en: “Amazon is always our top priority,” noted an Armen Living marketing analyst: “…
sentences.zh: “亚马逊始终是我们的首要任务，”Armen Living 营销分析师指出：“......

sentences.en: If the problematic retail does not fix by the end of the week, we will discontinue from your problematic competition to ensure that Amazon can return to a healthy state with these items.”
sentences.zh: 如果有问题的零售在本周末没有得到解决，我们将停止与你们有问题的竞争，以确保亚马逊能够通过这些商品恢复健康状态。”

blocks.html_en: In the end, Armen Living confirmed in an email to Amazon that it had removed its inventory from the “offenders sites”.

sentences.en: In the end, Armen Living confirmed in an email to Amazon that it had removed its inventory from the “offenders sites”.
sentences.zh: 最终，Armen Living 在给亚马逊的一封电子邮件中证实，它已从“违规网站”中删除了其库存。

blocks.html_en: Nguyen, the Amazon employee, thanked her for her action, but complained that the prices of two other chairs, which she had also flagged, had “dropped” and “still” not recovered “to the original price”.

sentences.en: Nguyen, the Amazon employee, thanked her for her action, but complained that the prices of two other chairs, which she had also flagged, had “dropped” and “still” not recovered “to the original price”.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊员工阮对她的行动表示感谢，但抱怨说，她也标记过的另外两把椅子的价格已经“下降”，并且“仍然”没有恢复到“原来的价格”。

blocks.html_en: “Let me know if you need more time to adjust this,” Nguyen said.

sentences.en: “Let me know if you need more time to adjust this,” Nguyen said.
sentences.zh: “如果你需要更多时间来调整，请告诉我，”阮说。

blocks.html_en: Nguyen did not respond to requests for comment. Amazon and Armen Living did not respond to specific questions about this incident.

sentences.en: Nguyen did not respond to requests for comment.
sentences.zh: 阮没有回应置评请求。

sentences.en: Amazon and Armen Living did not respond to specific questions about this incident.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊和 Armen Living 没有回应有关此事件的具体问题。

blocks.html_en: Martin Heubel, a consultant for e-commerce brands who previously worked as an Amazon vendor manager, said Amazon’s compensation demands help entrench its power in the market.

sentences.en: Martin Heubel, a consultant for e-commerce brands who previously worked as an Amazon vendor manager, said Amazon’s compensation demands help entrench its power in the market.
sentences.zh: 曾担任亚马逊供应商经理的电子商务品牌顾问马丁·休贝尔 (Martin Heubel) 表示，亚马逊的补偿要求有助于巩固其在市场上的影响力。

blocks.html_en: “It’s not so much about the money that you recover. It’s more about the education of your suppliers,” said Heubel, who said he was speaking as a consultant who advises brands negotiating with Amazon and not from his experience as a former Amazon employee.

sentences.en: “It’s not so much about the money that you recover.
sentences.zh: “这与你追回的钱无关。

sentences.en: It’s more about the education of your suppliers,” said Heubel, who said he was speaking as a consultant who advises brands negotiating with Amazon and not from his experience as a former Amazon employee.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是对供应商的教育。”Heubel 表示，他是作为一名顾问为品牌与亚马逊谈判提供建议，而不是根据他作为亚马逊前员工的经历。

blocks.html_en: “If you go elsewhere, you will get punished … so think twice about it whether you really do.”

sentences.en: “If you go elsewhere, you will get punished …
sentences.zh: “如果你去其他地方，你会受到惩罚......

sentences.en: so think twice about it whether you really do.”
sentences.zh: 所以请三思而后行，是否真的这样做。”

blocks.html_en: Amazon has said in legal pleadings that the incidents cited in the California case are “alleged to have occurred many years in the past” and involve “employees who lack pricing authority” and “a microscopic portion of Amazon’s catalog of millions of products”.

sentences.en: Amazon has said in legal pleadings that the incidents cited in the California case are “alleged to have occurred many years in the past” and involve “employees who lack pricing authority” and “a microscopic portion of Amazon’s catalog of millions of products”.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊在法律诉状中表示，加州案件中提到的事件“据称发生在很多年前”，涉及“缺乏定价权的员工”和“亚马逊数百万种产品目录中的一小部分”。

blocks.html_en: Confidential presentations

sentences.en: Confidential presentations
sentences.zh: 机密演示

blocks.html_en: The pressure Amazon exerts on its suppliers may only be partially visible in internal company emails like these because of Amazon’s efforts to train staff on how to avoid putting certain messages in writing, according to a recent court filing by the California attorney general.

sentences.en: The pressure Amazon exerts on its suppliers may only be partially visible in internal company emails like these because of Amazon’s efforts to train staff on how to avoid putting certain messages in writing, according to a recent court filing by the California attorney general.
sentences.zh: 根据加州总检察长最近提交的一份法庭文件，亚马逊对其供应商施加的压力可能只能在此类公司内部电子邮件中部分体现出来，因为亚马逊努力培训员工如何避免以书面形式传达某些信息。

blocks.html_en: The internal records obtained in the California lawsuit include an in-house presentation from 2019. It instructs staff to send screenshots of rivals’ product listings to suppliers when making compensation requests, but to avoid mentioning the companies’ names.

sentences.en: The internal records obtained in the California lawsuit include an in-house presentation from 2019.
sentences.zh: 在加州诉讼中获得的内部记录包括 2019 年的内部演示。

sentences.en: It instructs staff to send screenshots of rivals’ product listings to suppliers when making compensation requests, but to avoid mentioning the companies’ names.
sentences.zh: 它指示员工在提出赔偿要求时将竞争对手产品清单的屏幕截图发送给供应商，但避免提及公司名称。

sentences.en: Excerpt from 2019 Amazon internal presentation released in court by the California attorney general.
sentences.zh: 加州总检察长在法庭上发布的 2019 年亚马逊内部演示文稿摘录。
